observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to require
the (a) Inventory Committee to collaborate with all heads of local offices
for the documentation of PPEs with the end view of completing the
physical count of PPEs and render the corresponding RPCPPE to the
Accounting Office and the Office of COA Auditor; (b) Municipal
Accountant to (i) continue completing and updating PPELCs and property
cards, respectively;(ii) regularly reconcile PPE records; and (d) Disposal
Committee to facilitate the identification and disposition of unserviceable properties in conformance with the prescribed procedures.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) Municipal
Accountant to: (i) fully record the RPT/SET Receivables based on the
Certified List of Taxpayers and Tax Due (CLTTD) at the beginning of the
year; and (ii) to prepare the necessary entries to record the unrecognized
delinquent tax of ₱217,449,931; and (b) Municipal Treasurer to ensure
the timely preparation and updating of the CLTTD and provide the same
to the Accounting Office.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Department Heads
to strive in order to further decrease the cash deficit through introduction
of cost-cutting measures and limiting of expenditures to the essential
operating needs of the Municipality.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) accountable
officers to settle immediately all their cash advances which are already
due for liquidation; (b) Municipal Accountant to (i) strictly enforce the
rules in the granting, utilization, and liquidation cash advances cited in
COA Circular No. 97-002; (ii) issue demand letters to those who have not
liquidated their cash advances; and (iii) submit a request for write off of
dormant cash advances to the COA; and (c) Legal Officer to initiate legal
procedures to establish the official status of the missing AOs and to make
appropriate actions way forward.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the Municipal
Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to (a) cause the appropriate
adjustments of the reconciling items in the books of accounts; (b)
coordinate and reconcile the Cashbook and SL balances regularly for
immediate identification and recording of variances/errors for accurate
and fair presentation of the financial statements; and (c) prepare and
submit the monthly RAAF to facilitate timely monitoring of movements
of accountable forms.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the (a) BAC members to
(i) ensure that the required documents and procedures in procurement
are properly followed; (ii) properly plan the implementation of priority
projects under 20% Development Fund; and (b) Municipal Accountant
to submit the missing documents necessary to further verify/validate
transactions.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the: (a) MDRRMO to
continue the optimal utilization of the LDRRMF; (b) Chairman, Bids and
Awards Committee (BAC) to strictly adhere to the procurement
procedures and documentation requirements stipulated in Republic Act
No. 9184 to guarantee that all procurement activities comply fully with
legal and procedural standards to maintain transparency and
accountability; and (c) Municipal Accountant to ensure that all necessary
documentations are attached to the disbursement vouchers prior to the
release of payment to validate the legitimacy and accuracy of claims,
preventing any errors or discrepancies in financial transactions.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the I-REAP Head to:
(a) oversee the operations of the cooperative and introduce timely
interventions, if the circumstances warrant, with the view of ensuring that
the objectives of the project are met; (b) follow-up the preparation and
submission of the signed and notarized recovery plan by the cooperative;
(c) to immediately submit said plan to the RPCO to seek approval; (d)
closely monitor the status of the Hog Fattening Project and provide
immediate interventions to prevent further incurrence of losses which
may lead to the permanent closure of the subproject; (e) enforce the
recovery of the farm equipment/inputs listed in the inventory established
by the RPCO during the field monitoring; (f) remind the cooperative
officials of the possible implication of non-repayment/return of the
granted funds or goods in accordance with Section 5.2 of the Guidelines
for Non-Operational Subprojects; and (g) to follow-up on the status of
evaluation/review of the termination documents submitted by the other
PG.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the GAD Focal Point
personnel to continue promoting gender equality through identifying and
implementing GAD responsive PPAs addressing significant gender issues
within the Municipality.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal
Treasurer and BPLO to ensure the continuous execution of effective
collection strategies towards collection performance efficiency.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Accountant to: (a)
promptly recognize and record all leave benefits payable, in accordance
with the provisions outlined in DBM Budget Circular No. 2013-01, COA
Circular Nos. 2024-009 and 2012-001, and P.D. No. 1445; (b) work closely
with the HRMO and the Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that updated
leave cards for all active employees are reconciled and enough
appropriations for leave credits are allocated; (c) ensure that the
disbursement vouchers are supported with complete documentation in
compliance with Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-
001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) Human
Resource Management Officer to (i) prioritize the filling of vacant
plantilla positions, particularly those essential to the daily operations of
various departments; (ii) explore the possibility of regularizing qualified
and long-serving Job Order or Casual personnel, subject to CSC’s merit
and fitness; and (iii) prepare and maintain personnel files, particularly
contract of services with clearly defined duties and responsibilities of
hired Job Order Personnel to avoid confusion and duplication of duties;
and (b) Municipal Accountant to ensure that the supporting documents
were attached to the disbursement vouchers to establish validity and
propriety of payment.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal
Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to continue the timely
withholding and remittance of taxes.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to; (a) reconcile with the GSIS their
respective records, and adjust balances as warranted; and (b) continue
observing the requirements of R.A. No. 8291 or the GSIS Act of 1997 on
the regular deduction and timely remittances of premiums and loan
amortizations.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to: (a) coordinate with the
Philhealth Office for the reconciliation and proper disposition of
unreconciled balances of Due to PhilHealth; and (b) to continuously
comply with the National Health Insurance Act on the withholding and
prompt remittance of PhilHealth premiums.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer (a) to continuously comply with the
requirements of R.A. No. 9679 or the Home Development Mutual Fund
Law of 2009 on the withholding and prompt remittance of HDMF
contributions and loan repayments; and (b) coordinate with the HDMF
Office for the reconciliation and proper disposition of their unreconciled
balances.

We recommended and the LCE concurred to require the settlement of
audit disallowances as these become due, in accordance with the
prescribed procedures and regulations.

observations.managementAction:
Officers and employees that are active in the service with existing cash advances/disallowances were directed to settle or liquidate their cash advances, 
likewise, the accounting office will enforce salary deductions from their monthly salary and other remuneration if any and seek for legal assistance for appropriate action.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the:

 (a) Municipal Treasurer to allocate adequate time for his duties as the GSO of the municipality;

 (b) Municipal Accountant to complete the PPELCs and account the municipality’s real properties through the RPLCs; and

(c) Inventory Committee to complete the physical count of PPEs.
observations.managementAction: Management commit that they will have a creation of Supply Officer under Office of the Mayor to perform and maintain adequate records all Property and Plant and

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the:

(a) RHU to (i) coordinate with the PHO/distribution hub (Science City of Muñoz) for the valuation of the unissued vaccines as at December 31, 2022; and 

(ii) sustain its efforts until the desired immunity of the people is attained;

(b) MAO to (i) recognize in the books the value of the unused vaccines as at year-end; and 


(ii) henceforth ensure that all donated items are recorded in the books of accounts; and 

(c) RHU and MAO to maintain their respective records on vaccine inventories in accordance with prescribed procedures.
observations.managementAction: Management commit to the Creation and Procurement of additional health staff and functionaries.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed that the LCE direct the head of MPMIU to assist the PG in seeking alternative profitable solution to sustain its farm machineries rental operations, thus, ensuring the continued provision of benefits to the farmer beneficiaries. 
We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the: 

(a) MPMIU to (i) facilitate coordination of the non-operational PGs with the oversight bodies of PRDP, particularly on matters involving dissolution of cooperatives and the cancellation of enterprise agreements;

(b) PSO to assist in the preparation of recovery plan for the hog-fattening project and other similarly situated cooperatives in accordance with Sections 1 and 2 of the guidelines for Non-operational I-REAP.

We also advise the LCE to:

(a) consider the LGU’s possible takeover of the two non-operational Sub-projects in accordance with the prescribed procedures; 

(b) consider the possible transfer of the project to another capable and qualified PG in conformance with the PRDP set guidelines without prejudice to requiring the officers of the defunct PGs to reimburse the PRDP of the losses it incurred as a result of apparent mismanagement of the Sub-projects, if warranted; and 

(c) return to the General Fund of the LGU the unused cash in bank balances pertaining to the PGs and LGU’s funding counterparts.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and Human Resource Management Officer to:

 (a) regularly update the GSIS of changes in salaries and employment status of personnel;

 
(b) reconcile with the GSIS their respective records, and adjust balances as warranted; and 

(c) continue observing the requirements of R.A. No. 8291 or the GSIS Act of 1997 on the regular deduction and timely remittances of premiums and loan amortizations.
observations.managementAction: Management fully complied with the said Act.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer:

(a) coordinate with the Philhealth Office for the reconciliation and proper disposition of unreconciled balances of Due to Philhealth; and

(b) to continuously comply with the National Health Insurance Act on the withholding and prompt remittance of Philhealth premiums.
observations.managementAction: Management fully complied with the said Act.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer:

 (a) to continuously comply with the requirements of R.A. No. 9679 or the Home Development Mutual Fund Law of 2009 on the withholding and prompt remittance of HDMF contributions and loan repayments and 

(b) coordinate with the HDMF Office for the reconciliation and proper disposition of their unreconciled balances.
observations.managementAction: Management fully complied with the said Act.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LCE concurred to require the settlement of audit disallowances as these become due, in accordance with the prescribed procedures and regulations.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to require the (a) Inventory Committee to: collaborate with all heads of local offices for the documentation of PPEs with the end view of completing the physical count of PPEs and render the corresponding RPCPPE to the Accounting Office and the Office of COA Auditor;(b) Municipal Accountant to recognize in the books the unrecorded real property; (c) Municipal Accountant and Inventory Committee to (i) continue completing and updating PPELCs and property cards, respectively;(ii) regularly reconcile their PPE records; and (d) Disposal Committee to facilitate the identification and disposition of unserviceable properties in conformance with the prescribed procedures.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) accountable officers to settle immediately all their cash advances which are already due for liquidation; (b) Municipal Accountant to (i) strictly enforce rules in the granting, utilization, and liquidation cash advances cited in COA Circular 97-002;  (ii) remind or issue demand letters to those who have not liquidated their cash advances on time; and (iii) request for write off of unliquidated cash advances for those that are eligible for write off subject to COA rules and regulations on the writing off of accounts; and (d) Legal Officer to initiate the taking of legal steps in establishing the official status of the missing AOs and to make appropriate actions way forward.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) BAC members to ensure that the required documents and procedures of procurement are properly followed; and (b) Municipal Accountant to submit the missing vouchers and required documents for further verification/validation of the transactions.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the: (a) MDRRMO to ensure the preparation of monthly report using the required format and timely submission of the same; (b) Chairman, Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to ensure that the necessary procurement process and documentation in compliance with RA 9184 are strictly observed; and (c) Municipal Accountant to ensure that all necessary documentations are attached to the disbursement vouchers prior to the release of payment to ensure the validity of the claims.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the I-REAP Head to: (a) continue monitoring the condition of the farm equipment and the result of operations, and introduce timely interventions, if the circumstances warrant, with the view of ensuring that the objectives of the project are met; (b) closely monitor the status of the Hog Fattening Project and provide immediate interventions to prevent the further incurrence of losses; otherwise, recommend for the termination of the project in the manner provided in the Enterprise Investment Agreement and Implementation Management Agreement; (c) communicate with the other PGs concerned to establish the actual status of the cooperatives to date; (d) follow-up on the submission of the required documentation needed for the proper closure/termination of the PRDP subproject; and (e) require the submission of the status of funds transferred to the cooperatives to establish their accountability for the PRDP funds received.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to immediately deposit all collections as often as necessary, and thereafter, strictly comply the instruction of Section 32 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
We further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant (a) cause the appropriate adjustments of the reconciling items in the books of accounts; and (b) coordinate and reconcile the Cashbook and SL balances regularly for immediate identification and recording of variances/errors for accurate and fair presentation of the financial statements.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the MSWMB to (a) strengthen the role of the Barangays in solid waste management by strictly enforcing the composting operation in the MRFs and ensure that they are fully operational and functional to lessen the tipping fees paid by the Municipal Government; and (b) provide revenue generating measures for the end products of biodegradable and recyclable waste materials.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct its GAD Focal Point personnel to continue promoting gender equality through identifying and implementing GAD responsive PPAs addressing significant gender issues within the Municipality.

We commended Management for its efforts in surpassing its collection targets for CY 2023 and recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer and BPLO to ensure the continuous execution of effective collection strategies towards collection performance efficiency.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to continue in abiding with the BIR regulations by withholding and timely remitting all the taxes due and payable to the government.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to; (a) reconcile with the GSIS their respective records, and adjust balances as warranted; and (b) continue observing the requirements of R.A. No. 8291 or the GSIS Act of 1997 on the regular deduction and timely remittances of premiums and loan amortizations.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer (a) to continuously comply with the requirements of R.A. No. 9679 or the Home Development Mutual Fund Law of 2009 on the withholding and prompt remittance of HDMF contributions and loan repayments and (b) coordinate with the HDMF Office for the reconciliation and proper disposition of their unreconciled balances.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer (a) coordinate with the Philhealth Office for the reconciliation and proper disposition of unreconciled balances of Due to Philhealth; and (b) to continuously comply with the National Health Insurance Act on the withholding and prompt remittance of Philhealth premiums.
We recommended and the LCE concurred to require the settlement of audit disallowances as these become due, in accordance with the prescribed procedures and regulations.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer in coordination with the Municipal Engineer to continue:

 (a) the Municipality’s satisfactory compliance with regulations on the allocation and use of the 20% DF; and 

(b) assessing new developments that affect the socio-economic and environmental status of the community as inputs in future developmental planning for a more responsive application of the fund.
observations.managementAction: Management ensure that the 20% DF conforms with the implementing rules and regulations.

observations.recommendation:
We commended Management for its efforts in surpassing its collection targets for CY 2022 and recommended to which the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to:

(a) ensure the continuous execution of effective collection strategies; and

(b) consider the viability of using the BLGF targets in the annual budget to further challenge the LGU in looking for new or better approaches towards enhanced collection performance efficiency.
observations.managementAction: Management provided equipment, trainings and seminar to employees assigned in this office to enhance their capabilities and strategies in revenue collection.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the MDRRMC to:

(a) draw a plan on the use of available LDRRMF, be it current, continuing or STF, based on an extensive evaluation of the vulnerability of the Municipality to natural or man-made disaster risks; 

(b) ensure that the LDRRMF is optimally utilized; and 

(c) sustain the current effective responses to disasters and its attendant risks.
observations.managementAction:
MDRRMO were closely monitoring and ensuring the readiness of the Municipality in handling disasters.

The LDRRMF were mostly utilized handling MDRRMO expenses and disaster-related expenses.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the GAD Focal Person to continue its compliance with its mandate of promoting and providing a gender-responsive environment in the local government.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality fully implemented the PPAs on the approved GPB.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to continue in abiding with the BIR regulations by withholding and timely remitting all the taxes due and payable to the government.
observations.managementAction: Management fully complied with the said Act.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the GAD focal point to make necessary adjustment in the planning for and implementation of GAD PPAs to ensure that GAD issues as still properly considered and championed through mainstreaming in COVID-19 related activities or until the situation normalizes.
observations.managementAction: Almost all PPAs were realigned to other activities so such action did not contribute in addressing/ promoting gender equality, women’s empowerment, and gender-responsive local governance.

observations.recommendation: 1.15	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to (a) continue sustaining its effective responses for any emerging natural or man-made risk and to act on it accordingly; and (b) MHO to ensure accuracy in the capture of details of vaccinees being uploaded in the DOH website.

2.10	We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the head of MPMIU to assist the PG in seeking alternative profitable uses of its farm machineries thus, sustaining its operation and ensuring the continued provision of benefits to the farmer beneficiaries.
2.17	We recommended and the LCE concurred to (a) coordinate with the Sangguniang Bayan for the passing of a resolution operationalizing the shifting of the ASF status of the locality that would allow the resumption of hog industry in the area; and (b) direct the MPMIU to (i) advise the PG of Hog Fattening Project to resume production once the related enabling ordinance is passed; (ii) hold accountable the PG of the Goat Production and Marketing Project for the apparent mismanagement of their enterprise pursuant to the provisions of the EIA; and (iii) instruct the PG of Tomato Production to seek advice from the  PRDP Regional Project Coordinating Office on what  appropriate action to take in view of the imminent dissolution of the Cooperative vis-à-vis  the requirements if the EIA is cancelled.

3.7	We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) require the Municipal Treasurer/Acting GSO and the Municipal Accountant to prioritize the updating and reconciliation of property records; and (b) re-create an Inventory Committee and assign an overall focal person that will conduct physical count of all property, plant, and equipment and render a report thereon to the Municipal Accountant, copy furnished the COA Audit Team.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to continue (a) sustaining its effective responses to disaster-related risks; and (b) being on the lookout for any emerging natural or man-made risk and to act on it accordingly.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) Municipal Engineer to ensure that unfinished projects are completed as planned; and (b) Local Development Council (LDC) to continue on finding ways on how to align future development projects with the demands of the times with requisite consideration of applicable regulations.

6.8	We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the GAD Focal Person to consider the viability of the PPAs included in the next year’s GPB considering the updated situation of the COVID-19 pandemic by then, and take appropriate actions with the end view of maximizing the use of fund set aside for GAD.
7.7	We recommended and the LCE agreed to maintain its good collection efforts and continue to provide innovative collection strategies so as to further improve revenue collection.

8.7	We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to continue in diligently abiding with the BIR regulations by withholding and timely remitting all the taxes due and payable to the government.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to (a) continue in observing the requirements of R.A. No. 8291 or the GSIS Act of 1997 on the prompt payment of GSIS contributions and GSIS loans; and (b) reconcile with GSIS their respective records; and adjust balances as warranted.

10.4	We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to (a) continuously comply with the National Health Insurance Act on the withholding and prompt remittance of Philhealth premiums; and (b) disclose in the notes to FS the reason of the unremitted Due to Philhealth, and monitor the amount so eventually, the unremitted balance will be fully remitted.

11.3	We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to (a) continuously comply with the requirements of R.A. No. 9679 or the Home Development Mutual Fund Law of 2009 on the withholding and prompt remittance of HDMF contributions and loan repayments; and (b) reconcile with HDMF on the balance and eventually remit the remaining ₱96,591.67 or whatever the reconciled balance on Due to Pag-IBIG account.
12.4	We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the immediate settlement of audit disallowances pursuant to prescribed procedures and regulations.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to: 

(a) instruct the MSWD Officer to (i) ensure that profiling of beneficiaries of future assistance is meticulously done to preclude erroneous payments; and 

(ii) seek the DSWD’s approval prior to making payment to beneficiaries not included in their originally-approved list; and 

(b) sustain its pro-active responses in addressing the challenges of the COVID-19 pandemic.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to effectively respond to the COVID-19 pandemic as called for in the Bayanihan Grant; 

(b) the Municipal Accountant see to it that the required documents for each claim are submitted/attached to the DVs before these are paid in accordance with Section 2 of PD 1445 and other pertinent rules and regulations in the disbursement of funds.

observations.managementAction: No donation in kind for CY 2021. Prior year’s donations were still unrecorded.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the: 

(a) Municipal Accountant see to it that the required documents for each claim are submitted/attached to the DVs before these are paid; and 

(b) BAC to ensure that transparency of government procurements is strictly observed.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to encourage the LDRRM Council to continue: 

(a) sustain its effective responses to disaster-related risks; and 

(b) be on the lookout for any emerging natural or man-made risk and act on it accordingly especially during this time of health crisis.
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to keep up on its good collection efforts and continue to provide innovative collection strategies so as to further improve revenue collection.
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to continue to comply with BIR regulations on the withholding and prompt remittance of taxes.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to continue to adhere to the requirements of R.A. No. 8291 or the GSIS Act of 1997 on the prompt payment of GSIS contributions and GSIS loans and thus allow the GSIS members timely access to the services and benefits being provided by the System.
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to continuously comply with the National Health Insurance Act on the withholding and prompt remittance of Philhealth premiums.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to continuously comply with the requirements of R.A. No. 9679 or the Home Development Mutual Fund Law of 2009 on the withholding and prompt remittance of HDMF contributions and loan repayments.
We recommended the Local Chief Executive to require the immediate settlement of audit suspensions and disallowances pursuant to prescribed procedures and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The management was able to settle all of the outstanding Notice of Suspensions issued during previous years amounting to ₱12,766,679.99

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to (a) instruct the MSWD Officer to (i) ensure that profiling of beneficiaries of future assistance is meticulously done to preclude erroneous payments; and (ii) seek the DSWD’s approval prior to making payment to beneficiaries not included in their originally-approved list; and (b) sustain its pro-active responses in addressing the challenges of the COVID-19 pandemic.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to effectively respond to the COVID-19 pandemic as called for in the Bayanihan Grant; (b) the Municipal Accountant see to it that the required documents for each claim are submitted/attached to the DVs before these are paid in accordance with Section 2 of PD 1445 and other pertinent rules and regulations in the disbursement of funds.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor concurred to instruct the (a) Municipal Accountant to (i) report and disclose in the quarterly and year-end financial statements the status of donations-in-kind received;(ii) the offices concerned for the submission of report to the oversight bodies on any cash and in-kind donations officially received from the donors; (b) Property Officer to maintain adequate records for donations-in-kind and submit to the Municipal Accountant a status report thereof as at end of every quarter; and (c) Municipal Accountant, MDRRMO Head and the Property Officer to henceforth coordinate for the proper accounting of cash and in-kind donations in accordance with applicable rules and regulations.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the (a) Municipal Accountant see to it that the required documents for each claim are submitted/attached to the DVs before these are paid; and (b) BAC to ensure that transparency of government procurements is strictly observed.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to encourage the LDRRM Council to continue (a) sustain its effective responses to disaster-related risks; and (b) be on the lookout for any emerging natural or man-made risk and act on it accordingly especially during this time of health crisis.
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to keep up on its good collection efforts and continue to provide innovative collection strategies so as to further improve revenue collection.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the GAD focal point to make necessary adjustment in the planning for and implementation of GAD PPAs to ensure that GAD issues as still properly considered and championed through mainstreaming in COVID-19 related activities or until the situation normalizes.
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to continue to comply with BIR regulations on the withholding and prompt remittance of taxes.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to continue to adhere to the requirements of R.A. No. 8291 or the GSIS Act of 1997 on the prompt payment of GSIS contributions and GSIS loans and thus allow the GSIS members timely access to the services and benefits being provided by the System.
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor concurred to continuously comply with the National Health Insurance Act on the withholding and prompt remittance of Philhealth premiums.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to continuously comply with the requirements of R.A. No. 9679 or the Home Development Mutual Fund Law of 2009 on the withholding and prompt remittance of HDMF contributions and loan repayments.
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the immediate settlement of audit suspensions and disallowances pursuant to prescribed procedures and regulations.

observations.managementAction: Management committed to (a) deduct from the salaries of AOs still employed their unliquidated cash advances; and (b) request from COA for an authority to write off those Cash Advances aged 10 years and above.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to:

(a) require the Municipal Treasurer/Acting GSO and the Municipal Accountant to prioritize the updating and reconciliation of property records; and 

(b) re-create an Inventory Committee and assign an overall focal person that will conduct physical count of all property, plant, and equipmentand render a report thereon to the Municipal Accountant, copy furnished the COA Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: They promised to fully comply with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to continue sustaining all the programs and activities of the LDRRM for the benefit of the community it serves.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to continue the satisfactory implementation of GAD programs, projects and activities in ensuring GAD-responsive public governance.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the Sanggunian Bayan to work together for a timely enactment of budget/appropriations in the ensuing years to allow the timely implementation of development projects.

We recommended that the LCE and the SB to work towards an optimized delivery of public service through the timely enactment of the municipality’s annual budget to sustain the satisfactory implementation of RA 9003 or the Solid Waste Management (SWM) Act of 2000.

Further, we advised that the procurement of the solid waste procurement facility be conducted to significantly address the solid waste management problem of the Municipality.

We commended that the Local Chief Executive to continue in achieving and even surpassing its revenue collection targets and to keep up its effective income collection strategies so that funds for the delivery of public service is made available.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the: 

(a) Municipal HRMO to ensure that pertinent contracts are issued to every job order/contract of service personnel before payments are made;

(b) Municipal Accountant to ensure that supporting documents are complete before payment; and 

(c) facilitate the submission the required supporting contracts in settlement of the notice of suspension.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to continue complying with BIR regulations by remitting all taxes withheld to the BIR.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to continue adhering to the requirements of R.A. No. 8291 or the GSIS Act of 1997.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to continuously comply with the National Health Insurance Act.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to continue complying with the requirements of R.A. No. 9679 or the Home Development Mutual Fund Law of 2009.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to require the immediate settlement of audit suspensions and disallowances pursuant to prescribed procedures and regulations.
observations.managementAction: Management promised to submit the technical inspection required to satisfy COA requirements.

observations.recommendation: 1.12	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (a) undertake all legal actions available to management to enforce the liquidation of cash advances; (b) instruct the Municipal Accountant to request for write off all unliquidated cash advances that are eligible for write off in accordance with COA rules and regulations on the writing off of accounts.
3.7	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to continue sustaining all the programs and activities of the LDRRM for the benefit of the community it serves.
4.7	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to continue the satisfactory implementation of GAD programs, projects and activities in ensuring GAD-responsive public governance.

5.7	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive and the Sanggunian Bayan agreed to work together for a timely enactment of budget/appropriations in the ensuing years to allow the timely implementation of development projects.

6.5	We recommended and the LCE and the SB agreed to work towards an optimized delivery of public service through the timely enactment of the municipality’s annual budget to sustain the satisfactory implementation of RA 9003 or the Solid Waste Management (SWM) Act of 2000.Further, we advised that the procurement of the solid waste procurement facility  conducted significantly address the solid waste management problem of the Municipality.

7.4	We commended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to continue in achieving and even surpassing its revenue collection targets and to keep up its effective income collection strategies so that funds for the delivery of public service is made available.

8.9	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the (a) Municipal HRMO to ensure that pertinent contracts are issued to every job order/contract of service personnel before payments are made;(b) Municipal Accountant to (a) ensure that supporting documents are complete before payment; and (c) facilitate the submission the required supporting contracts in settlement of the notice of suspension.
9.4	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to continue complying with BIR regulations by remitting all taxes withheld to the BIR.
10.4	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed tocontinue adhering to the requirements of R.A. No. 8291 or the GSIS Act of 1997.
11.4	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to continuously comply with the National Health Insurance Act.
12.3	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to continue complying with the requirements of R.A. No. 9679 or the Home Development Mutual Fund Law of 2009.
13.5	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the immediate settlement of audit suspensions and disallowances pursuant to prescribed procedures and regulations.

observations.managementAction: Demand letters were sent to accountable officials

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

(a) require the Municipal GSO and the Municipal Accountant to prioritize the updating and reconciliation of property records; and 

(b) create an Inventory Committee that will conduct physical count of all property, plant, and equipment and render a report thereon to the Municipal Accountant, copy furnished the COA Audit Team.
observations.managementAction:
Management replied to fully comply with the recommend-dation

Management replied that an inventory committee team will be recreated following the death of the chair of earlier inventory committee created.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and Sanggunian Bayan ensure that succeeding appropriation ordinances are timely passed to enable the execution of LDRRM PPAs or all other programs and activities of the Municipality for that matter, for the benefit of the community it serves.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and Sangguniang Bayan facilitate the timely passing of Appropriation Ordinance so as not to hamper the delivery of public service, those pertaining to gender and development included.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the Sangguniang Bayan facilitate the delivery of public service through the timely enactment of its annual budget. 

Further, we suggested that the LCE require the Budget Officer to (a) charge to the GF the loan amortization for the construction of the Municipal Building; and 

(b) ensure the charging of expenses to the 20% Development Fund in accordance with DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2010 – 138 dated December 2, 2010 and Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 of the DILG and DBM.

We recommendedthat the LCE and the SB work towards an optimized delivery of public service through the:

(a) timely enactment of the municipality’s annual budget; and 

(b) seeking of alternative actions in resolving the SWM issues particularly on waste collection and disposal.
observations.managementAction: The budget for 2019 was enacted only on the 4th quarter of 2019 thus, not all activities were accomplished.

observations.recommendation: We commended management for achieving and even surpassing its revenue collection targets during the past two years and further recommended to which management agreed that they continue keeping up its effective income collection strategies so as to generate more resources to fund the delivery of public service.

We recommendedthat the LCE instruct the MENRO in coordination with concerned barangay officials and the Provincial Mining Regulatory Board (PMRB) of Nueva Ecija to:

(a) monitor the quarry operations in their respective barangays; 

(b) report to the PMRB quarry operators who are extracting quarry materials (i) beyond the volume stated in the hauling slip; and (ii) outside of the allowed quarry sites. 

Likewise, require the Municipal Accountant to (i) validate the correctness of the share that the Municipality is receiving from the Province, in accordance with the legislated rates and reported volume of quarry extractions; and 

(ii) accrue in the books income shares from quarry operations that are not remitted to the Municipality as at end of the reporting period.Further we recommended that Sangguniang Pambayan craft related ordinance or the LCE to issue Executive Order regulating quarry operations.
We recommendedthat the Municipality continue to comply with BIR regulations by remitting all taxes withheld to the BIR.
We recommended that the Municipality continue following the requirements of Republic Act No. 8291 or the GSIS Act of 1997.
We recommended to continuously comply with the National Health Insurance Act by deducting and remitting all contributions due to the Philippine Health Insurance Corporation.
We recommended to continue observing the requirements of R.A. No. 9679 or the Home Development Mutual Fund Law of 2009.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the immediate settlement of audit suspensions and disallowances pursuant to prescribed procedures and regulations.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive and Sanggunian Bayan ensure that succeeding appropriation ordinances are timely passed to enable the execution of LDRRM PPAs or all other programs and activities of the Municipality for that matter, for the benefit of the community it serves.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive and Sangguniang Bayan facilitate the timely passing of Appropriation Ordinance so as not to hamper the delivery of public service, those pertaining to gender and development included.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive and the Sangguniang Bayan facilitate the delivery of public service through the timely enactment of its annual budget. Further, we suggested that the LCE require the Budget Officer to (a) charge to the GF the loan amortization for the construction of the Municipal Building; and (b) ensure the charging of expenses to the 20% Development Fund in accordance with DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2010 – 138 dated December 2, 2010 and Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 of the DILG and DBM.

We recommended and management agreed that the LCE and the SB work towards an optimized delivery of public service through the (a) timely enactment of the municipality’s annual budget; and (b) seeking of alternative actions in resolving the SWM issues particularly on waste collection and disposal.

We commended management for achieving and even surpassing its revenue collection targets during the past two years and further recommended to which management agreed that they continue keeping up its effective income collection strategies so as to generate more resources to fund the delivery of public service.

We recommended and management agreed that the LCE instruct the MENRO in coordination with concerned barangay officials and the Provincial Mining Regulatory Board (PMRB) of Nueva Ecija to (a) monitor the quarry operations in their respective barangays; (b) report to the PMRB quarry operators who are extracting quarry materials (i) beyond the volume stated in the hauling slip; and (ii) outside of the allowed quarry sites. Likewise, require the Municipal Accountant to (i) validate the correctness of the share that the Municipality is receiving from the Province, in accordance with the legislated rates and reported volume of quarry extractions; and (ii) accrue in the books income shares from quarry operations that are not remitted to the Municipality as at end of the reporting period. Further we recommended that Sangguniang Pambayan craft related ordinance or the LCE to issue Executive Order regulating quarry operations.
