observations.reason: Reiterated in 2018 AAR under Observation No. 1

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: 

(a) apply effective cash programming to facilitate the proper handling of cash such that it is available when needed for payment of obligations and at the same time, optimize its utilization; and 

(b) adopt austerity measures on disbursements to improve the cash position of the Municipality.

With the foregoing, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the: 

(a) the DRRM Officer and/or MPDC officer to prepare and complete data base on the basic/minimum information on vulnerable sectors i.e., Community Based Monitoring System (CBMS)/Rapid Community –Based Monitoring System (RCBMS) as well as update said CBMS to properly assess plan and manage disaster risks; 

(b) the DRRM Officer and MPDC to consider maintaining the volume of stock pile procured through public bidding in compliance to RA 9184; and 

(c) the HRM Officer to provide for additional two permanent staff in the DRRM Office to comply with the three staff structure as required under the minimum standards for disaster risk reduction and management preparedness.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the GAD focal person and GAD TWG in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer put into effect the prescribed GAD mechanisms and processes such as the establishment of GAD Database, adoption and formulation and preparation of appropriate GAD plan and budget in accordance with the provisions of the JMC.
observations.reason: Reiterated in 2018 AAR under Observation No. 4

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE require the: 

(a) members of the Municipal’s Local School Board and the Budget Officer to prepare a balanced budget with conscious and concerted efforts of observing compliance with the legal and regulatory requirements on SEF budgeting, allocation and utilization and refrain from accommodating expenditures for activities for which the DepEd has the direct mandate to execute; 

(b) the Local School Board and the Superintendent regularly monitor the utilization of SEF and implementation of PPAs and take appropriate actions on any deviations, if necessary; and 

(c) the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the DepEd Superintendent to ensure the proper and complete documentation of SEF expenditures to support its occurrence and validity to avoid suspensions and disallowances in audit.

We recommended that in the absence of any feasible alternative, the LCE secure authority from the Sanggunian Bayan for the renewal of the contract with Metro Clark Waste Management Corporation (MCWMC) to address waste disposal problem and prevent exposing to danger the public’s health, and to be in adherence to the mandate of Republic Act No. 9003.  

Meanwhile, we advise management to refund to MCWMC the excess amount received from the latter which represented services rendered for the month of December 2016.
observations.reason: Reiterated in 2018 AAR under Observation No. 6

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: 

(a) Budget Officer to ensure the proper charging and allocation of the 20% Development Fund in accordance with DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2010 – 138 dated December 2, 2010 and Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 of the DILG and DBM; and 

(b) charge to the GF the loan amortization pertaining to that applied for the construction of the Municipal Building and other future similar charges not partaking the nature of development and capital investments.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the: 

(a) Local Poverty Reduction and Action Team (LPRAT), as the program’s oversight body to (i) coordinate with the funding agencies to facilitate satisfaction of their requirements; (ii) closely monitor the project implementation for the timely identification of concerns that may affect the effective and efficient use of the funds received; and 

(b) Municipal Engineer oversee the projects in accordance with the technical specifications required by the funding agencies and clearly defined in the contract.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the drivers/officials concerned to: 

(a) accomplish properly and completely the Driver’s Trip Ticket on a per trip basis; 

(b) prepare the Monthly Report of Official Travels and the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption; and 

(c) submit the same to the COA Auditor within the ensuing month.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the: 

(a) concerned officials and the Municipal Accountant to submit the required documents to prove the regularity of said payments;

(b) Head of Offices to exercise prudence in the utilization of their representation allowances in procuring meals and snacks during meetings and other activities, and to submit fully documentation thereof; and 

(c) Municipal Accountant being the LGU’s internal auditor observe the limitations and requirements of such expenditures as prescribed by regulations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the Municipal Planning Development Officer to: 

(a) install a Full Disclosure Bulletin Boards in at least two publicly accessible and conspicuous places in the Municipality; 

(b) post thereon the missing five reports and the reports on fund utilization for TF, SEF and 20% DF and the QSCF as of the fourth quarter of CY 2017; and 

(c) henceforth observe the timelines in the posting of the required operational reports at the designated places in order to attain maximum transparency in LGU governance.

We recommended to intensify collection on RPT SET, other regulatory fees and service user charges/service income. Moreover, keep up on its good work of increasing revenue collections of the Municipality.
We recommended that the Municipality continue to comply with BIR regulations by remitting all taxes withheld to the BIR.
We recommended that the Municipality continue adhering to the requirements of R.A. No. 8291 or the GSIS Act of 1997.
We recommended that the Municipality continue to comply with the National Health Insurance Act by remitting all contributions withheld to the Philippine Health Insurance Corporation.
We recommended to continue adhering to the requirement of R.A. No. 9679 or the Home Development Mutual Fund Law of 2009.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the immediate settlement of audit suspensions and disallowances pursuant to prescribed procedures.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendations that the:

(a) Municipal Treasurer furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly signed certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible every beginning of the year; and 

(b) Municipal Accountant draw a JEV to record the RPT/SET receivables based on the certified list from the Office of the Municipal Treasurer.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

require the (i) Municipal Accountant to maintain updated PPELC for each class of property to record the acquisition, adjustments and disposal thereof;  and
 
(ii) Municipal Treasure/Property Officer maintain complete and updated property cards for all PPE purchased in prior and current year and ensure to update them regularly to avoid backlogs;  

create an Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of property, plant, and equipment by type annually and submit to the Auditor concerned not later than January 31 of each year; and
 
require the Municipal Treasurer to issue corresponding ARE for every PPE assigned to accountable officers.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) instruct concerned officials to exert more effort to obtain records/documents on the all real properties of the municipality and complete database of information thereon and to secure adequate insurance for all insurable properties of the municipality.

1.10.	We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Mayor (a) require the Municipal GSO and the Municipal Accountant to prioritize the updating and reconciliation of property records; and (b) create an Inventory Committee that will conduct physical count of all property, plant, and equipmentand render a report thereon to the Municipal Accountant, copy furnished the COA Audit Team.

2.13.	We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a) accountable officers to settle immediately all their cash advances which are due for liquidation; (b) Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to withhold the salaries of accountable officers who will fail to comply with the directive or demand for liquidation; (c) Municipal Accountant to strengthen the internal control over the granting of cash advances by all officials concerned to ensure the proper granting, utilization and liquidation of all cash advances in accordance with the existing rules and regulations; (d) Municipal Accountant to intensify monitoring of unliquidated cash advances such that the Accountant regularly give reminders or otherwise issue demand letters to those who have not liquidated their cash advances and  went beyond the  reglementary period on which to liquidate; and (e) Municipal Accountant to make effort to include in his action plan the request for write off of unliquidated cash advances for those that are eligible for write off subject to COA rules and regulations on the writing off of accounts.

3.5.	We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive (a) apply effective cash programming to facilitate the proper handling of cash such that it is available when needed for payment of obligations and at the same time, optimize its utilization; and (b) adopt austerity measures on disbursements to improve the cash position of the Municipality.

5.3.	We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Mayor instruct the GAD focal person and GAD TWG in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer put into effect the prescribed GAD mechanisms and processes such as the establishment of GAD Database, adoption and formulation and preparation of appropriate GAD plan and budget in accordance with the provisions of the JMC.

6.3.	We recommended and management agreed that the LCE require the (a) members of the Municipal’s Local School Board and the Budget Officer to prepare a balanced budget with conscious and concerted efforts of observing compliance with the legal and regulatory requirements on SEF budgeting, allocation and utilization and refrain from accommodating expenditures for activities for which the DepEd has the direct mandate to execute; (b) the Local School Board and the Superintendent regularly monitor the utilization of SEF and implementation of PPAs and take appropriate actions on any deviations, if necessary; and (c) the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the DepEd Superintendent to ensure the proper and complete documentation of SEF expenditures to support its occurrence and validity to avoid suspensions and disallowances in audit.

7.7.	We recommended and management agreed that in the absence of any feasible alternative, the LCE secure authority from the Sanggunian Bayan for the renewal of the contract with Metro Clark Waste Management Corporation (MCWMC) to address waste disposal problem and prevent exposing to danger the public’s health, and to be in adherence to the mandate of Republic Act No. 9003.  Meanwhile, we advise management to refund to MCWMC the excess amount received from the latter which represented services rendered for the month of December 2016.

8.7.	We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Mayor require the (a) Budget Officer to ensure the proper charging and allocation of the 20% Development Fund in accordance with DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2010 – 138 dated December 2, 2010 and Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 of the DILG and DBM; and (b) charge to the GF the loan amortization pertaining to that applied for the construction of the Municipal Building and other future similar charges not partaking the nature of development and capital investments.

9.7.	We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive require the (a) Local Poverty Reduction and Action Team (LPRAT), as the program’s oversight body to (i) coordinate with the funding agencies to facilitate satisfaction of their requirements; (ii) closely monitor the project implementation for the timely identification of concerns that may affect the effective and efficient use of the funds received; and (b) Municipal Engineer oversee the projects in accordance with the technical specifications required by the funding agencies and clearly defined in the contract.

10.8.	We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive direct the drivers/officials concerned to (a) accomplish properly and completely the Driver’s Trip Ticket on a per trip basis; (b) prepare the Monthly Report of Official Travels and the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption; and (c) submit the same to the COA Auditor within the ensuing month.

11.8.	We recommended and management agreed that the LCE require the (a) Municipal GSO to procure from the PS-DBM all common-use supplies and equipment; (b) stop concerned officials and employees to, except on emergency situations, perform purchasing function otherwise lodged to the GSO; (c) obtain from DBM-PS Certificates of Non-Availability of Stocks to support the procurement from local based suppliers; and (d) consider prices offered by the DBM-PS in making purchases from other suppliers to obtain the most reasonable prices for procured items.

12.6.	We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive require the (a) concerned officials and the Municipal Accountant to submit the required documents to prove the regularity of said payments; (b) Head of Offices to exercise prudence in the utilization of their representation allowances in procuring meals and snacks during meetings and other activities, and to submit fully documentation thereof; and (c) Municipal Accountant being the LGU’s internal auditor observe the limitations and requirements of such expenditures as prescribed by regulations.

13.5.	We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the Municipal Planning Development Officer to (a) install a Full Disclosure Bulletin Boards in at least two publicly accessible and conspicuous places in the Municipality; (b) post thereon the missing five reports and the reports on fund utilization for TF, SEF and 20% DF and the QSCF as of the fourth quarter of CY 2017; and (c) henceforth observe the timelines in the posting of the required operational reports at the designated places in order to attain maximum transparency in LGU governance.

14.5.	We recommended and management agreed to intensify collection on RPT SET, other regulatory fees and service user charges/service income. Moreover, keep up on its good work of increasing revenue collections of the Municipality.
15.4.	We recommended and management agreed that the Municipality continue to comply with BIR regulations by remitting all taxes withheld to the BIR.
16.3.	We recommended and management agreed that the Municipality continue adhering to the requirements of R.A. No. 8291 or the GSIS Act of 1997.
19.4.	We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive require the immediate settlement of audit suspensions and disallowances pursuant to prescribed procedures.

observations.managementAction: We strictly enforce the liquidation of cash advances. Those who failed to liquidate on time, the unliquidated amount were deducted from their salaries.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:
Municipality observe strictly the general limitations on personal services cited under Section 325(a) of RA 7160 and Item 4.2 of LBC No. 98 dated October 14, 2011;

the Municipal Mayor, being the Local Chief Executive, to be always guided by the conditions of the agency’s annual and supplemental budgets and ensure that the budgeting requirements were completely complied with; and 

the Municipal Mayor to have a political will to allow only the payment of personal services costs within the personal services limitation to avoid excessive payments.

We recommended that the management undertake the following measures:

establish its policies on the recruitment of JO/Contact of Service and reduce their number based on the necessity of the Municipality or as the situation warrants;

instruct the HRMO to (a) adhere to the existing laws, rules and  regulations with respect to the screening and qualification of applicants and execution of MOA  or job order contracts; and (b) regulate, evaluate and supervise the accomplishment and submission of necessary papers evidencing the performance of duties/functions or works assigned to individual casual  and job order personnel; and

advise the Accountant to ensure that all payrolls and payments thereof are properly supported with complete documentation.

We recommended that:

Municipal Mayor, Municipal Vice Mayor and the Sanggunian Members promulgate policies on subscription or set of controls that would justify the reasonableness and necessity of providing mobile phone loads to selected officials or the grant to incur mobile phone consumptions in consideration of existing COA regulations;  and

Municipal Mayor instruct the Accountant strictly comply with the documentary requirements prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-001 and ensure that all subsequent payments for communication expenses are supported with complete documentations to avoid suspensions or disallowances in audit.

We recommend that Management ensure that only eligible expenses were charged against the 20% Development Fund in accordance with DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2010 – 138 dated December 2, 2010 and Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 of the DILG and DBM.
We recommended that the Municipality refrain from using the LDRRM fund for purposes or activities other than those authorized under Section 21 of RA 10121.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to comply consciously with the timely reporting and submission of accounts and financial reports in order to avoid the postponement of the Auditor’s actions relevant to the subject transactions and other post audit activities.

We recommended  and the management agreed that the:

Municipality procure its common-use supplies from the PS – DBM in compliance with AO No. 17 and Section 53(5) of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184; and 

Municipal Mayor require the BAC to prepare and submit to the GPPB the Procuring Monitoring Report in printed and electronic format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester in compliance with Section 12.2 of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184.

We reiterate our recommendations that Municipal Local School Board   should:
 
strictly ensure the proper charging and allocation of disbursements to SEF, otherwise, ineligible transactions will be disallowed if warranted;

prepare a balanced budget with conscious and concerted efforts of observing compliance with the legal and regulatory requirements on SEF budgeting, allocation and utilization and refrain from accommodating expenditures for activities for which the DepEd has the direct mandate to execute; and

maximize the utilization of SEF to attain its objectives, monitor the implementation of the PPAs, and take appropriate actions such as realignment or reprograming, if necessary.

We recommended that Management fully adhere to the provisions under the Joint Circular, thus, we recommend that Management fully adhere to the provisions contained under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013 – 01  (JMC) issued by the Philippine Commission on Women PCW), Department of Interior and Local Government (DILG), Department of Budget and Management (DBM) and National Economic and Development Authority (NEDA) particularly on the establishment of GAD data base and preparation of GAD Plan and Budget of the Municipality.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the SWMO to:

to continue the close monitoring of barangays’ compliance with the implementation of existing environmental laws and regulations, and  maintain coordination with Barangay Officials to sustain the constituents’ obedience on “no segregation, no collection policy and the “no littering policy”; and

exercise strong political will to implement  the five basic environmental laws on preserving and protecting the environment required under R.A. No. 9003.

We recommended that the members of the SB consider:

the enactment of appropriate ordinance respecting the above DOTC’s and DILG’s directives and guidelines to prevent undesirable outcome of unregulated proliferation of tricycles along the national highways; and

the provision or designation of separate lanes in national highways for three-wheeled public conveyances in coordination with the proper government authorities and recommend its strict implementation.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive remit all taxes withheld promptly to the BIR as required by Section 1(c) of Republic Act No. 7649 known as the National Internal Revenue Code.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to remit all dues withheld promptly to the GSIS as required by Section 52 paragraphs g and h of Republic Act No. 8291.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the immediate settlement of disallowances, otherwise, cause the withholding of salaries of those employees determined to be liable and comply with the requirements asked in the Notices of Suspensions, otherwise, these will be mature into disallowances.

We reiterated the previous recommendation and management agreed to request for write-off of these accounts due to the absence of records and documents evidencing the claims and obligations of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: Management committed to take actions for write-off of dormant accounts

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer 

(a) strictly comply with the regulation on the rendition of accounts and financial reports in order not to delay in the verification and post-audit activities; and 

(b) observe the due dates of submission of the required financial reports and documents to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Lack of Manpower to implement the recommendations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive: 

(a) instruct the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly signed certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year; and 

(b) the Municipal Accountant to draw a JEV to record the debit to RPT/SEF tax receivables at the beginning of the year based on the certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year instead of mere income estimates.
observations.reason:
Not implemented | Reiterated in CY 2016 AAR as Finding No. 2

Difficulty in establishingcertified list of taxpayers/ lack of manpower

No duly certified list of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year was provided by the Municipal Treasurer

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendations and management agreed to: 

a) stop the granting of additional cash advances to officials and/or employees unless the previous advances are first settled or a proper accounting thereof is made; 

b) cause the withholding of salaries or any money due the officers and employees with unliquidated cash advances to compel the settlement/liquidation thereof; and 

c) request for write-off the unliquidated cash advances of officers and employees separated due to death.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive thru the in charge of the Solid Waste Management, the MPDC, to:

involve closely the Barangay Officials in the strict implementation on the existing environmental laws and intensify the information campaign on the “no segregation, no collection policy and the “no littering policy” among the constituents; and

exercise strong political will to implement  the five basic environmental laws on preserving and protecting the environment required under R.A. No. 9003.
observations.reason: Reiterated in CY 2016 AAR Finding No. 15

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the immediate settlement of disallowances, otherwise, cause the withholding of salaries of those employees determined to be liable and comply with the requirements asked in the Notices of Suspensions, otherwise, these will be mature into disallowances.
observations.managementAction: No action from the management to settle the NS and NDs during the year.
observations.reason: Reiterated in CY 2016 AAR Finding No. 19

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to (a) require the concerned personnel to liquidate/submit liquidation reports as soon as the purpose thereof had been accomplished and refund/return the excess cash advance, if any for the full settlement of cash advance; (b) update and monitor regularly the timely liquidation of cash advances and send demand letters to the personnel with outstanding cash advance; (c) refrain from accepting liquidation reports with unutilized portion of the cash advance not yet returned; (d) refrain from granting new cash advance to personnel without the liquidation of the previous cash advance; (e) cause withholding of the salary of the accountable officer if no liquidation of the cash advance was received; and (f) exert efforts in recovery and/or settlement of long overdue cash advances by separated officials and employees.
The Municipal Accountant commented that they started to deduct from the salaries of accountable employees certain amounts of unliquidated cash advances respecting the instruction or order of former Municipal Mayor upon the recommendation of the former Auditor of the LGU. He also sent demand letters to the concerned accountable officers before, however, the said officers refused to receive the demand letters.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendations that the (a) Municipal Treasurer furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly signed certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible every beginning of the year; and (b) Municipal Accountant draw a JEV to record the RPT/SET receivables based on the certified list from the Office of the Municipal Treasurer.
In the exit conference, the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Assessor committed to comply with the audit findings and will work out on the preparation of the certified list of taxpayers/assessment roll and the recording of the RPT and SET within this year.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
require the (i) Municipal Accountant to maintain updated PPELC for each class of property to record the acquisition, adjustments and disposal thereof;  and (ii) Municipal Treasure/Property Officer maintain complete and updated property cards for all PPE purchased in prior and current year and ensure to update them regularly to avoid backlogs;
create an Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of property, plant, and equipment by type annually and submit to the Auditor concerned not later than January 31 of each year; and
require the Municipal Treasurer to issue corresponding ARE for every PPE assigned to accountable officers.
During the exit conference, the Municipal Accountant stated that the Municipal Mayor requested for an inventory of the PPE assigned the each office, however to date, no reports were submitted by the department heads due to lack of records in their respective offices.
The Audit Team emphasized the need to issue corresponding Acknowledgment Receipt for Equipment (ARE) to accountable officers and be renewed every three years.

We recommended that the:
Municipality observe strictly the general limitations on personal services cited under Section 325(a) of RA 7160 and Item 4.2 of LBC No. 98 dated October 14, 2011;
the Municipal Mayor, being the Local Chief Executive, to be always guided by the conditions of the agency’s annual and supplemental budgets and ensure that the budgeting requirements were completely complied with; and
the Municipal Mayor to have a political will to allow only the payment of personal services costs within the personal services limitation to avoid excessive payments.

We recommended that the management undertake the following measures:
establish its policies on the recruitment of JO/Contact of Service and reduce their number based on the necessity of the Municipality or as the situation warrants;
instruct the HRMO to (a) adhere to the existing laws, rules and  regulations with respect to the screening and qualification of applicants and execution of MOA  or job order contracts; and (b) regulate, evaluate and supervise the accomplishment and submission of necessary papers evidencing the performance of duties/functions or works assigned to individual casual  and job order personnel; and
advise the Accountant to ensure that all payrolls and payments thereof are properly supported with complete documentation.
According to the Municipal HRMO, the Management committed to comply with the noted observations. They are currently evaluating the job qualifications and job descriptions of the said employees and revising their respective contracts. The accomplishments reports were completed and to be submitted to the Audit team. Moreover, he informed the Audit Team of the intention of the Municipal Mayor to reduce the number of job order personnel.

We recommended that:
Municipal Mayor, Municipal Vice Mayor and the Sanggunian Members promulgate policies on subscription or set of controls that would justify the reasonableness and necessity of providing mobile phone loads to selected officials or the grant to incur mobile phone consumptions in consideration of existing COA regulations;  and
Municipal Mayor instruct the Accountant strictly comply with the documentary requirements prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-001 and ensure that all subsequent payments for communication expenses are supported with complete documentations to avoid suspensions or disallowances in audit.
In their response to the corresponding Audit Observation Memorandum regarding the aforementioned observation, the Local Chief Executive commented that:
Reimbursement of telephone expenses amounting to ₱2,296.00.
These telephone expenses were consumed mostly by the LCE’s office, such as, but not limited to the executive assistant, secretaries, and drivers performing and representing assigned functions in his behalf including monitoring and for security purposes through cellular phone. These were used during the month of December of which the LDRRMC Office was also a recipient for a proper dispense of patients whenever emergency case exist.
Reimbursement of telephone expenses (for a period covering from August to September 2016 ) amounting to ₱25,000.00.
The prepaid cards were disseminated to and used by the LDRRMC Officer and his staff for their constant communication relevant to disaster preparedness being regarded as disaster prevention month; the PNP for communication and monitoring purposes in connection with peace and order implementation in this Municipality; the Public and Safety Office (POSO)  for a timely communication with the departments concerned for purposes of market safety maintenance; some barangay officials for project follow-up and for monitoring purposes in relation to national campaign against illegal drugs to ensure drug-free barangays; and for the LCE’s official calls to barangay officials of different barangays concerned relevant to program implementation, to POSO members for personal monitoring purposes involving safety of vendor’s goods in the market especially during night time; to provincial, regional, and national offices for project monitoring and program strategies consultation, and for municipal heads of offices including his staff for a constant communication due to lack of intercommunication means in all offices.
Reimbursement of telephone expenses amounting to ₱17,500.00.
The prepaid cards were disseminated to and used similarly by the above recipients including the LCE for the same reason above-specified. These were used during the months of July to August 2016. In the absence of intercommunication means in this Municipality’s entire offices, these circumstances became inevitable without the intention to violate any rule prescribed by law.
The Sangguniang Bayan Secretary, during the exit conference, informed the Audit Team of a drafted resolution made by the SB setting policies on communication expenses.

We recommend that Management ensure that only eligible expenses were charged against the 20% Development Fund in accordance with DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2010 – 138 dated December 2, 2010 and Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 of the DILG and DBM.
The Budget Officer, during the exit conference, commented that although she recognized the improper charging of the amortization of loan for the municipal building, she still included it under 20% DF in the Annual Budget since that was the practice in the previous years.
The Audit Team suggested that the concerned officials should revisit the standard budgeting regulations to ensure that only eligible expenses are charged against the said fund.

We recommended that the Municipality refrain from using the LDRRM fund for purposes or activities other than those authorized under Section 21 of RA 10121.
The Municipal Budget Officer stated that these expenses, which lacked proper documents certifying that the financial assistance, were related to the calamity during that period.
The Audit Team acknowledged the representation made by the Budget Officer, however, the transactions she mentioned were the actual samples wherein which the Audit Team included in its substantive testing. From there, we kept our stand and raised the impropriety of her claims.
The Audit Team further emphasized the complete documentation of transactions. Certification or declaration of state of calamity coming from the proper authority should be attached to the related expenses to support the validity of the claim charged to the said fund.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to comply consciously with the timely reporting and submission of accounts and financial reports in order to avoid the postponement of the Auditor’s actions relevant to the subject transactions and other post audit activities.
According to the Municipal Accountant, the failure of the Municipality to submit its financial reports and records within the prescribed period was due to the delayed recording of various transactions owing to the lack of personnel of the department. Nevertheless, he and the Municipal Treasurer committed to comply prospectively.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) instruct concerned officials to exert more effort to obtain records/documents on the all real properties of the municipality and complete database of information thereon and to secure adequate insurance for all insurable properties of the municipality.
According to the Municipal Budget Officer, the Municipality could not insure its properties due to insufficiency of budgeted funds. The amount was intended only for the agency’s buildings which was under a loan agreement with Land Bank. In order to conform with the audit findings, the municipality will supplement its budget to fill the deficiency.

We recommended  and the management agreed that the:
Municipality procure its common-use supplies from the PS – DBM in compliance with AO No. 17 and Section 53(5) of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184; and
Municipal Mayor require the BAC to prepare and submit to the GPPB the Procuring Monitoring Report in printed and electronic format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester in compliance with Section 12.2 of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184.

We reiterate our recommendations that Municipal Local School Board   should:
strictly ensure the proper charging and allocation of disbursements to SEF, otherwise, ineligible transactions will be disallowed if warranted;
prepare a balanced budget with conscious and concerted efforts of observing compliance with the legal and regulatory requirements on SEF budgeting, allocation and utilization and refrain from accommodating expenditures for activities for which the DepEd has the direct mandate to execute; and
maximize the utilization of SEF to attain its objectives, monitor the implementation of the PPAs, and take appropriate actions such as realignment or reprograming, if necessary.
In response, the Municipal Budget Officer stated that she already deleted in the CY 2017 budget the expenses identified to be ineligible to be charged to the said fund.

We recommended that Management fully adhere to the provisions under the Joint Circular, thus, we recommend that Management fully adhere to the provisions contained under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013 – 01  (JMC) issued by the Philippine Commission on Women PCW), Department of Interior and Local Government (DILG), Department of Budget and Management (DBM) and National Economic and Development Authority (NEDA) particularly on the establishment of GAD data base and preparation of GAD Plan and Budget of the Municipality.
During the exit conference, the Municipal HRMO, designated GAD focal point person, stated that they have already formulated the GAD Code of the Municipality. Moreover, they are currently working on the establishment of their GAD database.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the SWMO to:
to continue the close monitoring of barangays’ compliance with the implementation of existing environmental laws and regulations, and  maintain coordination with Barangay Officials to sustain the constituents’ obedience on “no segregation, no collection policy and the “no littering policy”; and
exercise strong political will to implement  the five basic environmental laws on preserving and protecting the environment required under R.A. No. 9003.
The Municipal MENRO informed the Audit team that starting on May 1, 2017 they will strictly implement their ordinance on solid waste management. The office had started to disseminate the information to the respective barangays and through flyers given to their constituents. The municipality recently signed a new contract with the same service provider for the collection and disposal of their garbage.

We recommended that the members of the SB consider:
the enactment of appropriate ordinance respecting the above DOTC’s and DILG’s directives and guidelines to prevent undesirable outcome of unregulated proliferation of tricycles along the national highways; and
the provision or designation of separate lanes in national highways for three-wheeled public conveyances in coordination with the proper government authorities and recommend its strict implementation.
During the exit conference, the Municipal Health Officer, being member of the Peace and Order Council (POC), cited the request from the DepEd to formulate guidelines in order to protect the welfare of the students commuting daily to schools. She also stressed the need to strictly implement existing ordinances by the municipality to be able to effectively regulate the operations of the public conveyances within their locality.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive remit all taxes withheld promptly to the BIR as required by Section 1(c) of Republic Act No. 7649 known as the National Internal Revenue Code.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to remit all dues withheld promptly to the GSIS as required by Section 52 paragraphs g and h of Republic Act No. 8291.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the immediate settlement of disallowances, otherwise, cause the withholding of salaries of those employees determined to be liable and comply with the requirements asked in the Notices of Suspensions, otherwise, these will be mature into disallowances.

We reiterated our recommendations and management agreed that they: 

a) prioritize the initiation of an extensive physical count of its property that would redound to a more reliable PPE accounts in the books; and 

(b) maintain property records to establish custodianship and to pinpoint accountability. 

We further recommended and they agreed that management:



 a) provide sufficient protection to its assets; and

 b) exercise due care in the custodianship of property.
observations.managementAction: The management has started to have AREs on the newly acquired properties during 2016

observations.reason: Reiterated in CY 2016 AAR Finding No. 13

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed to Municipality’s compliance on the provisions contained under COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2012 – 1 dated March 25, 2013.
observations.managementAction: The Management has already started to recognize the Trust Liability this CY 2017.
Management was able to gradually decrease the cash deficit thru:

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendations and management agreed to: 

a) prioritize the remittances of trust liabilities due to different agencies; 


b) refrain from using trust funds for other purposes to avoid delay in remittances to the agencies concerned; and 

c) hold the municipal treasurer liable for whatever interest, charges and penalties that may arise from the delayed remittance of such trust funds.
observations.managementAction: Management gradually paid the long outstanding taxes by installment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive remit all taxes withheld promptly to the BIR as required by Section 1(c) of Republic Act No. 7649 known as the National Internal Revenue Code.
observations.managementAction: Management gradually paid the long outstanding taxes by installment.
observations.reason: Reiterated in CY 2016 AAR Finding No. 17

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive remit all dues withheld promptly to the GSIS as required by Section 52 paragraphs g and h of Republic Act No. 8291.
observations.managementAction: Management gradually paid the long outstanding GSIS Contributions by installment.
observations.reason: Reiterated in CY 2016 AAR Finding No. 18

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed to fully adhere to the provisions under Paragraph C.6 (7) contained in Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013 – 01  (JMC) issued by the Philippine Commission on Women (PCW), Department of Interior and Local Government (DILG), Department of Budget and Management (DBM) and National Economic and Development Authority (NEDA).

We also recommended and the management agreed to adhere to the following mechanisms as provided for in Institutional Mechanisms to Implement the Magna Carta of Women as provided for in the Guidelines on the Localization of the Magna Carta of Women under the same JMC: a) Establishment and Maintenance of GAD Data Base; and b) Mainstreaming Gender Perspectives in Local Development Plans.
observations.reason: Reiterated in CY 2016 AAR Finding No. 14

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the provisions COA Circular No 2012 – 001 for complete documentations.
